title: Bronzeville & Black Metropolis
summary: Trace Black Metropolis through Bronzeville public memory, architecture, publishing, community arts, and the institutions that helped define Black Chicago.

stops.title: Victory Monument
stops.description: Open in Bronzeville at a memorial that grounds the route in Black achievement, public memory, and the civic self-definition of Black Chicago.

stops.title: The Forum
stops.description: Move into a landmark of gathering, performance, and neighborhood institution-building at one of Bronzeville's strongest public anchors.

stops.title: Chicago Bee Building
stops.description: Read Black publishing, enterprise, and architecture together at a building that captures how Bronzeville represented itself to the city and beyond.

stops.title: South Side Community Art Center
stops.description: Center Black artistic life and institutional continuity at a landmark that has kept creative practice alive on the South Side for generations.

stops.title: DuSable Black History Museum and Education Center
stops.description: Close with one of Chicago's strongest institutions for Black history, interpretation, and cultural stewardship.

title: Great Migration, Labor & Housing Struggle
summary: Follow Black Chicago through migration memory, the Defender, Ida B. Wells, public-housing struggle, and labor geography to see how the city was built under pressure.

stops.title: Monument to the Great Northern Migration
stops.description: Open at Bronzeville's migration monument, where the route starts with movement, aspiration, and the remaking of Black life in Chicago.

stops.title: Former Chicago Defender Building
stops.description: Turn to the Black press at the building that helped imagine Chicago for Southern readers and gave the Great Migration one of its strongest public voices.

stops.title: Ida B. Wells-Barnett House
stops.description: Add anti-segregation struggle, investigative power, and Black civic resistance at the home of one of Chicago's defining public voices.

stops.title: National Public Housing Museum
stops.description: Read housing policy, displacement, and urban survival at a museum that helps connect Chicago's Black neighborhood story to public-housing history.

stops.title: Packingtown Museum at The Plant
stops.description: Close in the old stockyards geography, where labor, industrial change, and the working city help explain the structural pressures around migration and neighborhood life.

title: South Side Arts, Music & Cultural Memory
summary: Move through South Side performance spaces, arts institutions, and public cultural memory to see how Black Chicago keeps creative life in circulation.

stops.title: Harold Washington Cultural Center
stops.description: Open on King Drive at a South Side performance venue that ties Black public culture to neighborhood presence and civic memory.

stops.title: Hyde Park Art Center
stops.description: Add contemporary art and long-running neighborhood creative life at one of the South Side's strongest public arts anchors.

stops.title: Logan Center for the Arts
stops.description: Move into university-linked performance and experimental art space where South Side cultural life meets new work and public programming.

stops.title: Stony Island Arts Bank
stops.description: Read Black archival imagination, contemporary practice, and cultural preservation together at one of the city's most distinctive creative institutions.

stops.title: South Shore Cultural Center
stops.description: Close at a lakefront cultural center where performance, public gathering, and neighborhood history extend the route into the South Side's broader civic landscape.

title: Black Political Power in Chicago
summary: Trace Black political power from Bronzeville public life and Operation PUSH to Harold Washington's civic legacy and the city buildings where Chicago power is exercised.

stops.title: Bronzeville Visitor Information Center
stops.description: Open in Bronzeville at a neighborhood gateway that frames Black Chicago through public memory, local pride, and civic self-definition.

stops.title: Operation PUSH Community House
stops.description: Move into organizing, advocacy, and Black public leadership at a site tied to one of Chicago's most recognizable civic-power institutions.

stops.title: Harold Washington Cultural Center
stops.description: Read Black municipal legacy through a cultural center that keeps Harold Washington's name and public memory present on the South Side.

stops.title: Harold Washington Library Center
stops.description: Add the citywide scale of Harold Washington's legacy at a civic institution that connects Black political leadership to public knowledge and municipal memory.

stops.title: Chicago City Hall
stops.description: Close at the center of city government, where Black political power, coalition-building, and the machinery of municipal decision-making meet.

title: Woodlawn, Washington Park & Neighborhood Change
summary: Read neighborhood change through Washington Park arts infrastructure, Woodlawn redevelopment, and the public landscapes where South Side memory and future planning collide.

stops.title: Washington Park Arts Incubator
stops.description: Open on Garfield at an arts anchor that reads neighborhood change through community-based cultural infrastructure rather than abstract planning language.

stops.title: Green Line Performing Arts Center
stops.description: Stay on the block to see how performance space and local creative investment become part of neighborhood stabilization and public life.

stops.title: Washington Park
stops.description: Move into the larger public landscape, where neighborhood memory, disinvestment, and cultural ambition meet in the South Side's shared open space.

stops.title: Jackson Park
stops.description: Read the South Side's public landscape through a park where preservation, redevelopment, and neighborhood identity remain in active conversation.

stops.title: Obama Presidential Center Site
stops.description: Close in Woodlawn at a redevelopment anchor that makes neighborhood change concrete through investment, expectation, and long-running local debate.
